Word Guess Project Instructions ⚠️ Instructions Disclaimer

Word Guess Project Instructions ⚠️ Instructions Disclaimer The purpose of guide is to help you write and test your code. It is important you do the steps in order. This guide will give you test codes you can copy and paste into your code file to simulate input/output. It should be placed after your code. If your code passes the test code you should get all ✅, else ❌. Look at errors to edit your code. Delete test code after you have successfully passed all checks. You are encouraged to attempt before using hints.

Glossary Quick links for revision for concepts that you will need to know before starting this project: A Arrays  Lists  Methods  Tuples  C Conditions If Statements  D Dictionaries  Methods  F File Handling  Read  Methods  Functions  Range  I Input/Prompts  L Loops For  While  O Operators Assignment  Comparison  Logical  Membership  S Strings Formatting  Methods

Step 1: Download your files️ This will download a .zip file, named yourName_WordGuessProject.zip with a folder of all the files you need for this project. It is recommended you save the zip file in the “Documents” directory. Right-click on .zip file to extract files. In the folder you are given a script.py file to write your code. Open this .py file in your desired python code editor. The words.txt file contains 5-letter words, with each word on a separate line. Only the words in this file are valid guesses for the user. It is important you do not move files out of this folder for your project to run. Download ZIP ️️

Step 2: Import Modules Import the random module so we can use it to get random words from our words.txt file.  Test Code import urllib.request url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ed-tq/Python-Word-Guessing-Console-Game/refs/heads/main/02-test.py" with urllib.request.urlopen(url) as response: code = response.read().decode("utf-8") exec(code)

Step 3: Define get_started function This function stores name of the word file in a variable. Asks for user name. Prints: Welcome to Wordle 101 [userName] ======================================================================== Rules You have 6 guesses to figure out the solution. All solutions are words that are 5 letters long. Letters that have been guessed correctly are displayed in uppercase. Letters that are in the word but have been guessed in the wrong location are displayed in lowercase. ======================================================================== Returns file name.  Hint Use variables to store input from prompts input(). Copy and paste if needed. You can use \n in long sentences to continue a sentence on a new line. [userName] should be the name entered by the user. ℹ️ More You can use f-string formatting to print a variable in a string. This works for any variable type (e.g. str, int).  Example print(f"Hello {variable_name}!")  Test Code import urllib.request url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ed-tq/Python-Word-Guessing-Console-Game/refs/heads/main/03-test.py" with urllib.request.urlopen(url) as response: code = response.read().decode("utf-8") exec(code)

Step 4: Define list_of_words function This function takes a list of words, read, as an argument Creates a new empty list, words_list. Loops over each item in read and adds to new list. Returns the new list.  Hint Use list method .append().  Test Code try: original = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"] new_list = list_of_words(original) if new_list == original: print("✅ Values match the original list") else: print("❌ Values do not match the original list") if new_list is not original: print("✅ Returned a new list (different object)") else: print("❌ Returned the original list (not a new list)") new_list.append("date") if "date" not in original: print("✅ Modifying new list does not change the original") else: print("❌ Modifying new list changed the original") except NameError: print ("❌ Function 'list_of_words' not defined")

Step 5: Define ’get_random_word` function This function takes a list words as an argument. The function picks a random position (index) in the list. Returns the word from the words list at that position.  Hint Use random.randrange() or random.randint() to get random position/number. Store this number in a variable. ℹ️ More .randrange(start, stop) or (stop) generates a number from start or 0 (default) to stop - 1 (start number inclusive, stop number not inclusive). .randint(a, b) generates a number from a to b (both numbers inclusive). Use len(words) to get the total number on items in the list words. Make sure the index number exists in the words list (index starts at 0 to len(words)-1). To return a word from the list of words, think about indexing/how to access an item from a list.  Example list[index]  Test Code try: words = ["apple", "banana", "cherry", "grape", "melon"] result = get_random_word(words) if result in words: print("✅ Returned word is in the list") else: print("❌ Returned word is not in the list") results = set() for i in range(10): results.add(get_random_word(words)) if len(results) > 1: print("✅ Function returns different words (randomness works)") else: print("⚠️ Only one word returned in 10 tries (might still be random, but unlikely)") except NameError: print("❌ Function 'get_random_word' not defined")
